headline: Investment Analysis: 19 Linden St, Brookline Village

recommendation.summary: Strong site fundamentals undermined by asking price. Viable at $4-4.5M or with density upside via 40B/MBTA overlay. Do not proceed at $5.95M under as-of-right zoning.

ratings.note: Irreplaceable: transit, schools, walkability, scarcity of large lots
Brookline new construction sells at $950-$1,400/sf; 29% YoY appreciation
At $336/sf of buildable area, margins are negative to break-even
Margins reach 18-22% — workable for experienced developer
18-month demo delay + community opposition; no LHD veto power
Could double units but politically difficult in Brookline; check safe harbor
MBTA Communities Act forcing upzoning in transit areas — may benefit this parcel

zoningParameters.densityFormula: 3,000 sf first unit + 1,000 sf each additional
zoningParameters.parkingRequirement: Confirm — typically 1.5-2 spaces/unit in Brookline M districts

financialScenarios.name: Scenario A: As-of-Right (14 units at $5.95M)
financialScenarios.verdict: Does not pencil. Negative to break-even margin.

financialScenarios.name: Scenario B: As-of-Right (14 units at $4.5M acquisition)
financialScenarios.verdict: Marginal. Acceptable if sale prices hit $1,200/sf.

financialScenarios.name: Scenario C: Luxury Adaptive Reuse (14 units at $4.5M)
financialScenarios.verdict: Workable if premium pricing holds. Historic character adds marketing value but raises construction costs.

financialScenarios.name: Scenario D: Chapter 40B (28 units at $5.95M)
financialScenarios.verdict: Best margin scenario. Requires comprehensive permit, political navigation, and 2+ year entitlement timeline. Verify Brookline 40B safe harbor status.

financialScenarios.name: Scenario E: 40B (28 units at $4.5M acquisition)
financialScenarios.verdict: Strong deal. Meets typical developer return thresholds. Requires 40B permit and negotiated acquisition.

carryingCosts.note: 18-month demolition delay adds significant carrying costs before construction can begin
carryingCosts.impact: Adds ~$712K to total project cost during 18-month demo delay period alone

riskMatrix.mitigation: Budget $700K+ in carry costs. Use delay period for design, permitting, community engagement.
Adaptive reuse strategy reduces opposition. Early community engagement. Preservation Commission consultation.
Historic renovation adds 15-25% cost uncertainty. New construction behind existing structure is more predictable.
Brookline has historically resilient pricing. Pre-sales can de-risk. Proximity to medical employment centers provides demand floor.
Verify current safe harbor status. If blocked, pursue as-of-right with price negotiation or MBTA overlay density.
Confirm parking ratio. Underground parking adds $50-75K/space. Transit proximity may support reduced parking special permit.
Phase I/II environmental assessment during due diligence. 1843 structure likely contains lead paint and possibly asbestos.

riskMatrix.risk: Interest Rate Increase During Hold Period
riskMatrix.mitigation: Lock construction financing early. Minimize pre-construction hold period.

opportunities.detail: Brookline is a rapid transit community required to zone for 6,990 multifamily units. If this parcel falls within the Section 3A overlay, density may increase beyond M-1.0 limits.
Federal Historic Tax Credit (20% of qualified rehabilitation costs) available for income-producing properties on the National Register. Massachusetts also offers a state HTC.
Large lots in Brookline Village are extremely rare. New construction with historic character commands top-of-market pricing.
Proximity to Longwood Medical Area (Harvard Medical, Dana-Farber, Brigham & Women's, Boston Children's) creates deep, recession-resistant buyer pool.

dueDiligenceChecklist.item: Confirm exact height limit (35 vs 40 ft) from Table 5.01
Check if parcel is within MBTA Communities Act Section 3A overlay
Verify Brookline Chapter 40B safe harbor status
Survey / confirm lot geometry and actual buildable envelope
Meet with Brookline Planning Department (pre-application conference)
Consult Brookline Preservation Commission on adaptive reuse approach
Review comparable recent Brookline Board of Appeals decisions in M districts
Confirm open space and usable open space per unit requirements
Research deed restrictions or easements on parcel
Investigate utility capacity (sewer, water, electrical) for multifamily
